CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-03-2019 Continued To Jun-26-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Margot P. Cutter, Esq., Cutter Law P.C., Telephone 916-290-9400, appearing for Plaintiff;
Mollie F. Benedict, Esq., Tucker Ellis LLP, Telephone 213-430-3399, appearing for Defendant Edwards Lifesciences, LLC, et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES, LLC, EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES CORPORATION, EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES(U.S.)INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendants Edwards Lifesciences, LLC, Edwards Lifesciences Corp., and Edwards Lifesciences (U.S.) Inc.'s demurrer to Plaintiff Nancy Romney's first amended complaint is overruled as to the first and second causes of action for strict liability manufacturing defect and negligence in manufacturing and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for failure to warn. Liberally construing the complaint, Plaintiff has identified federal regulations previously violated by Defendants and alleged to have been violated leading to the incident that injured Plaintiff. (FAC 13, 21, 31.) In addition, the first amended complaint alleges the components of the Sapien 3 that are defective because they were not manufactured consistent with specifications. (Id.) It also alleges that the surgeon who performed Plaintiff's surgery concluded that the balloon was defective as manufactured. (Id. 26.) Finally, it alleges that Defendants were aware of the manufacturing defects prior to Plaintiff's surgery, and recalled the product due to the defect. (Id. 29.) Although these allegations remain fairly general, they are sufficient on demurrer to plead a manufacturing defect claim sufficient to overcome a claim of preemption under the federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act. (See Coleman v. Medtronic, Inc. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 413, 436 ["[W]e conclude that it is premature to determine whether Coleman has alleged a state law requirement that is parallel to federal requirements so as to survive preemption. At the pleading stage, we can only conclude that the complaint alleges sufficient facts to state a cause of action for manufacturing defect, the issue of preemption will necessarily be addressed after Coleman has some opportunity to conduct discovery."].) Defendants may raise the issue again by a motion for summary judgment after Plaintiff has had the opportunity to conduct discovery. (**See Word Minutes for complete details**)

Order is signed

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2019 Continued To May-22-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Edwards Lifesciences, Llc, Edwards Lifesciences Corporation, Edwards Lifesciences(U.S.)Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES, LLC, EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES CORPORATION, EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES(U.S.)INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

Defendants Edwards Lifesciences, LLC, Edwards Lifesciences Corp., and Edwards Lifesciences (U.S.) Inc.'s demurrer to Plaintiff Nancy Romney's complaint is sustained as to the first cause of action for strict liability manufacturing defect, the second cause of action for negligence in manufacturing, and the third cause of action for failure to warn, with 30 days leave to amend. (see word minutes for full details)

A proposed order is submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
